name: Admission documentation
description: Pre-admission assessments, welcome packs, consent forms
current_process: A qualified member of the care home management team visits the prospective resident at their current location (home or hospital) to carry out a care needs assessment, gathering medical history, current health status, medications, allergies, dietary needs, mobility level, and specific care requirements. This information is recorded on paper forms or fragmented digital templates. Staff then manually compile a welcome pack containing house rules, fee schedules, complaints procedures, and information booklets. Consent forms for photography, data sharing, medication administration, and Do Not Resuscitate decisions are printed and signed in wet ink. All collected data is then manually re-entered into the care planning system to create the initial support care plan — a process prone to duplication, missed fields, and transcription errors. Personal belongings checklists are completed on admission day. The entire cycle from referral to a fully documented admission typically takes 5–10 working days.
time_spent: Estimated 6–10 hours per admission across the full workflow: 2–3 hours for the pre-admission visit and assessment, 1–2 hours compiling and personalising the welcome pack, 1–2 hours obtaining and filing consent forms, and 2–3 hours re-keying data into the care management system and creating the initial care plan. Registered managers in busy homes handling 3–5 admissions per month can spend 30–50 hours monthly on admission paperwork alone.
ai_solution: AI can automate and streamline multiple stages of the admission workflow: (1) Pre-populate assessment forms by extracting data from referral documents, hospital discharge summaries, and GP letters using document AI / OCR. (2) Auto-generate personalised welcome packs by merging resident-specific information (name, room, key contacts, dietary needs) into branded templates. (3) Enable digital consent capture with e-signatures on tablets, automatically filing and timestamping each form. (4) Draft the initial care plan from assessment data, flagging gaps and suggesting person-centred goals based on identified needs. (5) Use NLP to cross-check that all regulatory fields are completed before admission is finalised. CareVision already offers a digital pre-admission tool that automatically creates a Support Care Plan from assessment data. AI-powered systems like Flowtrics report processing admissions up to 40% faster with 75% fewer missing documents.
buyer_language: "We spend days chasing paperwork for every new resident — printing forms, re-entering the same information into different systems, and still worrying we've missed something. By the time the resident arrives, we're already behind on their care plan."
outcome_statement: New resident admissions fully documented in hours instead of days — with pre-populated care plans, digital consent forms, and personalised welcome packs generated automatically from assessment data.
regulatory_alignment: Maps to CQC Quality Statement: 'Assessing needs' under the Effective key question (Theme 1: Working with People). CQC expects providers to assess and review health, care, wellbeing, and communication needs with the person involved. Also touches on Safe (accurate medication records from day one) and Responsive (person-centred admission experience). Notably, 13 out of 19 local authorities assessed scored 'Requires Improvement' on the assessing needs quality statement, indicating this is a sector-wide weakness.
existing_tech_gap: Current care management platforms (Nourish, Person Centred Software, Log my Care, CareVision) offer digital care planning and some pre-admission templates, but most still require significant manual data entry. CareVision's pre-admission tool auto-creates a basic care plan from entered data, but does not extract information from external documents (discharge letters, GP summaries). No mainstream platform uses AI to pre-populate fields from referral documents, auto-generate personalised welcome packs, or intelligently flag missing assessment areas. AI adds unique value through document extraction, cross-referencing regulatory requirements, and eliminating duplicate data entry.
implementation_complexity: Requires integration — AI document extraction needs connectivity with the care home's existing care planning software (e.g., Nourish, PCS) and ideally access to referral document formats. Digital consent capture requires tablet hardware and e-signature compliance. Template generation for welcome packs is relatively plug-and-play.
proof_of_concept_availability: Partial. CareVision offers a working digital pre-admission tool in the UK market. Flowtrics (US-based) demonstrates AI-powered admission automation with published metrics (40% faster processing, 75% fewer missing documents). Jotform offers AI-powered patient registration agents. No fully integrated UK care home-specific AI admission solution with document extraction exists as a turnkey product yet, making this a strong opportunity for a bespoke AI build.
